slides.title: Creating Directories
slides.content:
# mkdir() – Make Directory

`mkdir(string $directory, int $permissions = 0777, bool $recursive = false): bool` creates a directory.

```php
mkdir('uploads');
```

Permissions are modified by the current umask. To create nested directories, set `$recursive = true`.

```php
mkdir('images/2025/march', 0777, true);
```

slides.title: mkdir Example
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$dir = 'backups/daily';
if (!file_exists($dir)) {
    if (mkdir($dir, 0755, true)) {
        echo "Directory created.";
    } else {
        echo "Failed to create directory.";
    }
} else {
    echo "Directory already exists.";
}
?>

slides.title: Reading Directories
scandir and glob Examples
Deleting Directories
Recursive Directory Deletion (Example)
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
function deleteDir($dir) {
    if (!file_exists($dir)) return true;
    if (!is_dir($dir)) return unlink($dir);
    foreach (scandir($dir) as $item) {
        if ($item == '.' || $item == '..') continue;
        if (!deleteDir($dir . DIRECTORY_SEPARATOR . $item)) return false;
    }
    return rmdir($dir);
}
deleteDir('old_folder');
?>

quizData.question: Which function returns an array of files in a directory?
quizData.explanation: scandir returns an array of all entries. glob also returns matches but with pattern.

quizData.question: rmdir() can remove a directory that contains files.
